hypothesis: If single-step retrosynthesis models primarily exploit dataset biases like template frequency and product similarity, then models trained on datasets enriched for common templates and similar products will achieve high top-k and round-trip accuracy on test sets with seen templates and similar molecules but will suffer marked performance drops on out-of-distribution reactions with unseen templates or low Tanimoto similarity, and increasing template diversity in training as well as using template-free architectures will mitigate this drop.
